<!-- Generated by `npm run build:context` (GRAMMAR.md §10). Do not edit by hand. -->

# Color utilities

Utilities are token-bound class grammar, not hard-coded values. They live in `@layer utilities`, so unlayered consumer CSS overrides them without `!important`.

- Pattern families use `base[-edge]-{value}`. Edges are logical (`block`, `inline`, `block-start`, `inline-end`) unless a family says otherwise.
- When a family lists responsive support, prefix its class with `tablet:`, `tablet-lg:`, `desktop:`, `desktop-lg:`, or `widescreen:`.
- Negative values use `neg-{value}` only where the family offers them.
- Values resolve through design tokens; use the documented grammar instead of writing raw CSS values.

## Color utilities

Ink (text), canvas (background), border-color, and fill utilities for all color surfaces: primitive palette, semi-semantic (brand/neutral/accent), and semantic (role × family × intensity × variant). All surfaces emit base classes and interaction-state prefixed forms (hover:, focus:, active:, disabled:).

### color-palette

Primitive palette color utilities. Maps ink/canvas/border/fill roles to the full palette step scale for every color family.

Grammar: `{role}-{family}-{step}` — roles: ink, canvas, border, fill · family: blue, butterfield, charcoal, cyan, deepcurrent, dusk, ember, gold, green, indigo, lavenderfield, merlot, mint, orange, pink, red, rosewater, sea, stone, teal, violet, yellow · step: 50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 950 · state prefixes: `focus:` `hover:` `active:` `disabled:`

### color-semi-semantic

Semi-semantic color utilities for brand, neutral, and accent-primary/secondary surfaces. Maps ink/canvas/border/fill roles to the step scale without the 'color-' prefix.

Grammar: `{role}-{family}-{step}` — roles: ink, canvas, border, fill · family: brand, neutral, accent-primary, accent-secondary · step: 50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 950 · state prefixes: `focus:` `hover:` `active:` `disabled:`

### color-semantic

Semantic color utilities with role × family × intensity × variant axes. Canvas sets both background-color and background; ink sets color; border sets border-color.

Grammar: `{role}-{family}{-intensity}{-variant}` — roles: canvas, ink, border · family: accent-primary, accent-secondary, action, app, brand, caution, critical, disabled, info, positive · intensity: optional, subtle, muted, emphasis · variant: optional, inverse · state prefixes: `focus:` `hover:` `active:` `disabled:`
